issues.title: spring-ai-retry should not require spring-webflux dependency
issues.body:
**Bug description**
`spring-ai-retry/pom.xml` contains `spring-webflux`. This is an invasive library (conflicts with spring-webmvc) and should not be added as a dependency without a good reason. There is no reason: no class from spring-webflux is imported. There is one import of `org.springframework.web.client.ResponseErrorHandler`, but it's provided by much smaller `spring-web`. So the dependency should be on spring-web instead.

**Environment**
Spring AI 1.0.0 Release

**Steps to reproduce**
Check spring-ai-retry/pom.xml

**Expected behavior**
No spring-webflux dependency

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
N/A (dependencies issue)

issues.title: When use @Autowire List<McpSyncClient>mcpSyncClients,  If one of the configured MCP servers is invalid, it will cause the system to fail to start!
issues.body:
When use @Autowire List<McpSyncClient>mcpSyncClients, 
If one of the configured MCP servers is invalid, it will cause the system to fail to start.

How can I avoid this situation?

issues.title: Diagram structured-output-api PropmptTemplate Typo
issues.body:
Hello everyone, 
I would like to point out a spelling mistake on the [structured-output-api.jpg](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/fbfc87e814c6d5d2382d7e5f9b045246731a10dd/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/images/structured-output-api.jpg) image. There the ``PromptTemplate`` is called ``PropmptTemplate``.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/acc3c40c-5fed-48b5-87b7-a59e1a93f4b3)

comments.body: Indeed, and I noticed that it seems the `spring-ai-doc` does not provide the source files for the flowcharts?
Yes, I have also already searched for the source file in the repository but did not find one.
